meaning: n.里根主义(指美国第四十任总统Ronald Reagan实行的政策) 对里根主义的信奉
examples: So I'd place blame for Obama's undoubted troubles on (a) half-measures in the crucial early months (b) unwillingness to articulate a clear break with Reaganism and all that.
How would Reaganism be remembered if it hadn't had that dose of schmoozy charm blended with tough guile?
The New Federalism was similar to what would later become Reaganism: tax cuts, more power to the states, welfare reform.
Mr Wallace claimed to have invented Reaganism, with its simple music for the masses, and there is some truth in this.
